the above rule does not require
that the use must be public use in
fact.


To legalize a constructive public
use would be the virtual abrogation
of the rule itself. Under. the .consti-
tuition of the United States that
can not be legalized.


That was the intention Of article
81 of our state constitution, which
declares that "the use of the waters
of this state for irrigation, mining
and manufacturing purposes shall
be deemed public USE" But, in
Tacoma Industrial Co. VS.. White
River Power Co., 80 Pac. $50, the
supreme court said: "A state is
powerless to declare, either by con-
stitutional provision or by statute,
use to be public which is essen
tally and inherently private" The
court said the article violates the
due process clause of the federal
constitution.


Now, the proposed constitutional
amendment relating to logging rail
roads is identical in principle, and
is intended to accomplish it's object
in the same way as article 81. Sec
ton 16 of article l declares that
